svg.title: CUTLASS organizes a GEMM as nested tiles from the whole device down to a single Tensor Core instruction
svg.desc: Four nested boxes. The outermost is the device level, the whole GEMM C equals alpha times A times B plus beta times C. Inside it is the threadblock tile, typically 128 by 128, which stages A and B in shared memory. Inside that is the warp tile, typically 64 by 64, the portion one warp computes. Innermost, highlighted green, is the MMA instruction, a Tensor Core operation such as 16 by 8 by 16, the actual hardware instruction. You configure each level through C++ template parameters. The point is that CUTLASS is composable: it exposes the same tiling hierarchy you built by hand in the matmul and tensor-core posts, as reusable building blocks tuned per GPU architecture.
svg.text: CUTLASS: the same tiling hierarchy you built, as building blocks
Each level is a template parameter. CUTLASS composes them and tunes per GPU architecture.
the whole GEMM: C = alpha (A · B) + beta C
~128 x 128 · stages A, B in shared memory
~64 x 64 · the part one warp computes
Tensor Core op, e.g. 16 x 8 x 16
This is exactly the device -> block -> warp -> instruction nesting from the matmul and tensor-core posts, packaged and reusable.
